name: Printing Press (Movable Type)
tagline: Mechanised impression of reusable type — mass duplication of text with repeatable fidelity
problem:
A scriptorium can duplicate a book beautifully, but each copy ties up a scribe for months. Ideas spread at the speed of handwriting — fine for liturgy, lethal for science and navigation when printers multiply faster than wars. Movable type attacks the bottleneck: cast many identical letters once, arrange them into any page, print, break apart, reuse.

The obstacle is precision economics: type must be hard enough to survive thousands of impressions yet soft enough to file; height must match within microns or impressions ghost and ink floods. Paper supply and ink tack must agree with metal and press speed.

Printing also amplifies error: a wrong letter becomes a thousand wrong letters. Editorial workflows — proof sheets, stop-press corrections — become technologies alongside the press itself.

Politically, cheap text rearranges who may read law, scripture, and sedition. The press is not only gears; it is an argument about who controls reproduction.
overview:
Punchcutters carve master letters in steel, strike them into copper matrices, and cast many type pieces in lead-antimony-tin alloys that solidify sharp and wear slowly. Compositors set lines in composing sticks, justify with spaces, lock the forme into a chase, and ink evenly with composition rollers or balls.

Screw presses apply firm even pressure across the platen; later cylinder presses raise speed. Paper grain direction aligns with pull to reduce tearing; dampening relaxes fibres for deeper impressions.

Typography encodes readability: x-height, contrast, and ligatures are ergonomic choices. Colour printing layers separate formes — registration becomes mathematics.

Digital printers inherited the same questions with different physics — toner fusion and inkjet drops — but the social shape remains: cheap copies change regimes.

principles.name: Viscosity and tack
principles.explanation: Ink must split cleanly between type surface and paper without picking fibres — temperature and pigment load tune rheology.

principles.name: Hardness matching
principles.explanation: Type metal softer than punch steel but harder than paper contaminants — otherwise letters mushroom.

principles.name: Impression pressure distribution
principles.explanation: Platen parallelism and packing paper shims equalise density — else outer lines fade.

principles.name: Justification geometry
principles.explanation: Word spacing and thin spaces absorb line-length slack — hyphenation rules are hyphenation algorithms.

rawMaterials.name: Type metal
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Lead-antimony-tin type metal from smelter chains; short-run polymer or wood type where metal casting is unavailable — expect faster wear

rawMaterials.name: Punch steel
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: High-carbon punch steel; later EDM for fine repairs where electrical gear exists
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Harden and temper; polish striking face

rawMaterials.name: Paper stock
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Rag or chemical pulp sheets with grain aligned to press pull; avoid glossy coated stock until inks match

buildSteps.title: Cut one perfect letter punch before casting anything
buildSteps.description: Work a single letter in tool steel under magnification; proof it on smoke-blackened paper until curves are true. Only then strike the matrix — a bad punch multiplies misery.
buildSteps.warningNote: Overhard steel snaps; overtempered steel dents

buildSteps.title: Cast a few sorts and test height
buildSteps.description: Pour type metal into a hand mould; file feet until the letter matches your gauge block exactly. Print trial lines — if some letters shine while others fade, height is wrong.
buildSteps.warningNote: Lead splash burns — leather apron and face shield

buildSteps.title: Build a rigid chase and furniture system
buildSteps.description: Cut reglet and furniture from seasoned wood or metal; learn locking patterns that resist spread under pressure. Label cases with readable diagrams so novices do not resort letters.

buildSteps.title: Mix ink to the press you actually own
buildSteps.description: Start stiff, add varnish drops until the ink threads cleanly from roller to type without filling counters. Record room temperature — tack changes with heat.
buildSteps.warningNote: Too loose ink fills counters and looks muddy

buildSteps.title: Print progressively, correcting once per forme
buildSteps.description: Pull a single proof, mark faults with standard proofreader marks, fix type, re-lock, proof again. Only then run edition sheets, stacking with dry interleaving paper.
buildSteps.warningNote: Wet stacks set off ink offset — rotate boards

history.event: Bi Sheng experiments with ceramic movable type in Chinese tradition.
Gutenberg Bible era printing with metal type and press in Mainz.

inventors.name: Johannes Gutenberg
inventors.contribution: European metal movable-type printing with oil ink and press

impact:
Printing collapsed the price of identical knowledge — law, scripture, and diagrams could disagree in public because multiple copies existed. It preconditioned scientific societies and newspapers.

Movable type’s social externalities include propaganda at scale — technology never prints only truth.

videos.description: Museum demonstrations of composition and pull.
